name: research
description: Rigorous reading and auditing of argument documents — papers, RFCs, design proposals, ADRs, whitepapers, technical blog posts, anything that carries a claim-evidence structure. dissect one or more documents into Gap/Claim/Mechanism/Evidence skeletons, untangle how a set of documents relate (lineage / clusters / contradictions / contested ground, with optional claim-framed positioning), critique a document adversarially into a referee report (claim↔evidence audit + self-attack), or run a provenance audit over your own documents (trace every load-bearing citation back to a verified source; quarantine the unread). A paper is the most common instance, not the definition.
